dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses abstract 'momentum signal' concept. Mapped to MACD crossovers as a standard momentum indicator: positive momentum = MACD > signal line, negative/reversal = MACD < signal line. Exit conditions include both momentum reversal and position loss (2% threshold as reasonable proxy for loss detection). Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to support both long and short momentum trades. Stop loss mechanism is implicit in the momentum reversal exit condition rather than a fixed percentage.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses abstract 'momentum signal' concept. Mapped to MACD crossovers as a standard momentum indicator: positive momentum = MACD > signal line, negative/reversal = MACD < signal line. Exit conditions include both momentum reversal and position loss (2% threshold as reasonable proxy for loss detection). Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to support both long and short momentum trades. Stop loss mechanism is implicit in the momentum reversal exit condition rather than a fixed percentage.

source_rule_text: Momentum strategies benefit from stop losses because a losing position signals momentum reversal, making the continuously updated momentum signal itself act as a de facto stop loss mechanism.
